content:
Just got back from three days in the interior of the Caatinga, on a field expedition to collect biometric data on the local avifauna. Six individuals across five species made it into our nets, and one coruja-buraqueira nearly did too, but she was sharp enough to slip out of the mist net before we could get to her. Can't say I blame her.
No phone service the entire time, and the wifi, somehow, had no actual internet behind it. Just us, the birds, and the caatinga.
Fun fact: the "caatinga", the name of the biome, comes from the tupi-guarani language and meants "white forest".
We stayed in a room with six bunk beds, I had a bottom bunk, which I'm counting as a small victory. The kitchen staff there were phenomenal, we ate incredibly well the whole time.
Took a detour to the dam in Petrolândia and crossed the bridge connecting Pernambuco to Bahia, that kind of landscape always makes me feel very small in a good way. There were goats there too, walking along the ridges of the canyon.
It was a wonderful trip. Also an exhausting one. The kind of thing I'm glad I did and in no hurry to repeat anytime soon.
In my scraps of free time I started watching Gachiakuta's first season, enjoyed it enough that I'm planning a proper review once I find the time for it.

tags.content: Class got cancelled today. I get to stay home without having to interact with anybody. Yippee!

This website has been a great creative outlet for me.
This semester has been very difficult and draining, a lot of social interacting that I did not originally anticipate.
Not that it is a bad thing, It just takes me some time to adjust and I do so enjoy having time free from socializing.

I dont really know where I am going with my life, for the most part.
I have plans, dreams and goals. How to go about achieving them is the biggest problem.
Everything is just so abstract and distant that it all elludes me.

This is something small that i have control over.
I quite enjoy it.

Thank you for reading and thank you for your time.
The website has progressed quite a lot already. I've even made a guestbook, so if you see this and have the opportunity, feel free to use it.
This is my first attempt at a post. I hope it works well.
